content.question: How would a shift in gimnázium entry age from 10/12 to a later point (e.g., age 14 or 16) interact with the 2023 unified teacher-employment law and the existing centralization of curriculum and textbook authority? Would this require additional legislative amendments to align employer discretion, instructional time, and capacity-planning authority?
content.reduction_path: Undertake a regulatory gap analysis comparing the 2023 employment law text, the Klebelsberg Centre operational directives, and relevant portions of Act CXC/2011 to identify whether a structural change to gimnázium entry would trigger conflicts or create ambiguities in employer, curriculum, or capacity-allocation powers. Consult with legal counsel from the Ministry of Education and representatives from school-maintainer associations (state, church, foundation) to identify practical implementation sequencing and legislative dependencies.
